briefdescription.para: Points of the cloud.
Default constructor for the Cloud class.

detaileddescription.para: Initializes an empty cloud with no points.

briefdescription.para: Constructs a new Cloud object with random positions and values.

parameterdescription.para: Number of points to generate.
Random seed used to generate the points. Using the same seed will produce the same set of points.
Bounding box within which the points will be generated. The bounding box is defined as {xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax}.

briefdescription.para: Constructs a new Cloud object based on a list of existing points.

parameterdescription.para: A vector of Point objects representing the cloud's points.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< float > &x, const std::vector< float > &y, float default_value=0.f)
memberdef.name: Cloud

briefdescription.para: Constructs a new Cloud object from lists of x and y coordinates.

parameterdescription.para: A vector of x coordinates for the points.
A vector of y coordinates for the points.
The default value assigned to each point. Defaults to 0 if not specified.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< float > &x, const std::vector< float > &y, const std::vector< float > &v)
memberdef.name: Cloud

briefdescription.para: Constructs a new Cloud object from lists of x and y coordinates with assigned values.

parameterdescription.para: A vector of x coordinates for the points.
A vector of y coordinates for the points.
A vector of values associated with each point.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< glm::ivec2 > &indices, const glm::ivec2 &shape, const glm::vec4 &bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: Cloud

briefdescription.para: Construct a point cloud from grid indices mapped to a bounding box.

detaileddescription.para: Each index is normalized by the grid shape and remapped to the given bounding box. The resulting points are stored as 3D positions (z = 1).

parameterdescription.para: Input grid indices.
Grid dimensions.
Bounding box (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax).

briefdescription.para: Add a new point to the cloud.

parameterdescription.para: The point to be added to the cloud.

briefdescription.para: Remove a point from the cloud.

parameterdescription.para: Index of the point to be removed.

briefdescription.para: Get the bounding box of the cloud.

simplesect.para: glm::vec4 The bounding box of the cloud in the format [xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax].

briefdescription.para: Calculates the centroid of a set of points.

detaileddescription.para: This function computes the center (or centroid) of a collection of points by averaging their coordinates. It sums up all the point coordinates and then divides the result by the total number of points to obtain the average position. The centroid represents the geometric center of the point cloud.

simplesect.para: Point The computed center, represented as a Point object, which contains the average (x, y) coordinates of the points.

briefdescription.para: Computes the indices of the points that form the convex hull of a set of points.

simplesect.para: std::vector<int> A vector containing the indices of the points that make up the convex hull, listed in order.

briefdescription.para: Get the values assigned to the points in the cloud.

simplesect.para: std::vector<float> A vector containing the values of all points in the cloud.

briefdescription.para: Get the maximum value among the points in the cloud.

simplesect.para: float The maximum value among the points.

briefdescription.para: Get the minimum value among the points in the cloud.

simplesect.para: float The minimum value among the points.

briefdescription.para: Get the x coordinates of the points in the cloud.

simplesect.para: std::vector<float> A vector containing the x coordinates of the points.

briefdescription.para: Get the concatenated x and y coordinates of the points in the cloud.

detaileddescription.para: This method returns a vector containing the x and y coordinates of all points in the cloud, arranged in the form [x0, y0, x1, y1, ...].

simplesect.para: std::vector<float> A vector containing the concatenated x and y coordinates of the points.

briefdescription.para: Get the y coordinates of the points in the cloud.

simplesect.para: std::vector<float> A vector containing the y coordinates of the points.

briefdescription.para: Find the index of the nearest point in the cloud.

detaileddescription.para: Computes the point whose (x,y) position is closest to xy using squared Euclidean distance.

simplesect.para: This implementation is not optimized and is intended for occasional single queries. It performs a linear search and is not suitable for large-scale or repeated nearest-neighbor queries.

parameterdescription.para: Query position

briefdescription.para: Set points of the using x, y coordinates.

parameterdescription.para: A vector containing the x coordinates of the points.
A vector containing the y coordinates of the points.

briefdescription.para: Set new values for the cloud points.

parameterdescription.para: A vector of new values to assign to the points.

briefdescription.para: Set a single value for all cloud points.

detaileddescription.para: This method assigns the same value to all points in the cloud.

parameterdescription.para: The value to assign to all points.

briefdescription.para: Set the values of the cloud points using values from an underlying array.

detaileddescription.para: This method assigns values to the cloud points by interpolating values from an input array. The positions of the cloud points are mapped to the array using the specified bounding box.

parameterdescription.para: The input array from which to derive the values.
The bounding box that defines the mapping from the cloud points' coordinates to the array's coordinates.

briefdescription.para: Sets point values based on their distance to the bounding box border.

detaileddescription.para: For each point in the cloud, this method computes the shortest distance to the edges of the given bounding box and stores it as the point's value.

parameterdescription.para: Bounding box in the format {xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax}.

briefdescription.para: Set the values of the cloud points based on the distance to the convex hull of the cloud.

detaileddescription.para: This method assigns values to the cloud points based on their distance to the convex hull of the cloud. The convex hull must be initialized using to_graph_delaunay() before this method is called to ensure the correct indices of the convex hull points are available.

briefdescription.para: Sets point values based on the distance to their nearest neighbor.
Get the number of points in the cloud.

simplesect.para: size_t The number of points in the cloud.

briefdescription.para: Clear all data from the cloud.
Print information about the cloud's points.
Randomize the positions and values of the cloud points.

parameterdescription.para: Random seed number for generating positions and values.
Bounding box within which the points will be randomized.

briefdescription.para: Remap the values of the cloud points to a target range.

parameterdescription.para: The lower bound of the target range.
The upper bound of the target range.

memberdef.argsstring: (float dx, float dy, uint seed, float dv=0.f)
memberdef.name: shuffle

briefdescription.para: Randomly perturbs the positions and values of all points in the cloud.

parameterdescription.para: Scale factor for the random displacement along the X-axis.
Scale factor for the random displacement along the Y-axis.
Seed value for the pseudo-random number generator, ensuring reproducibility.
Scale factor for the random displacement applied to the point's value component v.

briefdescription.para: Snap points to the bounding box edges and corners.

detaileddescription.para: Points within a tolerance distance from the bounding box edges are projected onto the closest edge. Afterwards, the closest point to each corner is snapped exactly to that corner.

parameterdescription.para: Bounding box defined as (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax).
Ratio used to compute the snapping distance relative to the average point spacing.

briefdescription.para: Loads point data from a CSV file into the Cloud object.

detaileddescription.para: This function reads a CSV file where each line contains either 2D (X, Y) or 3D (X, Y, Z) point data. The function automatically detects the dimensionality of the points based on the number of values per line. The loaded points are stored in the points member of the Cloud object.

parameterdescription.para: The path to the CSV file to be read.

simplesect.para: true if the file was successfully read and the points were loaded, false otherwise.
The CSV file must be well-formed, with each line containing either 2 or 3 comma-separated values. Lines with an unexpected number of values will cause the function to return false.
If the file cannot be opened or contains invalid data (e.g., non-numeric values), the function will log an error and return false.

briefdescription.para: Project the cloud points onto an array.

detaileddescription.para: This method projects the cloud points' values onto a given array, mapping their positions within the specified bounding box. The resulting array will be populated with the values of the points at their corresponding positions.

parameterdescription.para: The input array where the cloud points' values will be projected.
The bounding box that defines the mapping from the cloud points' coordinates to the array's coordinates.

briefdescription.para: See hmap::to_array.

memberdef.argsstring: (Array &array, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f}, InterpolationMethod2D interpolation_method=InterpolationMethod2D::ITP2D_DELAUNAY, Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox_array={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f}) const
memberdef.name: to_array_interp

briefdescription.para: Interpolate the values of an array using the cloud points.

detaileddescription.para: This method populates an array with interpolated values based on the positions and values of the cloud points. The interpolation method can be specified, and optional noise arrays can be used for domain warping.

parameterdescription.para: The output array that will be populated with interpolated values.
The bounding box that defines the cloud's coordinate system.
The method used for interpolation (e.g., nearest neighbor, bilinear).
Optional reference to a noise array applied to the x-coordinates for domain warping (not in pixels).
Optional reference to a noise array applied to the y-coordinates for domain warping (not in pixels).
The bounding box of the destination array.

briefdescription.para: Export the cloud data to a CSV file.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the output CSV file.

briefdescription.para: Convert the cloud to a graph using Delaunay triangulation.

simplesect.para: Graph The resulting graph from Delaunay triangulation.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string &fname, int cmap, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f}, int depth=CV_8U, glm::ivec2 shape={512, 512})
memberdef.name: to_png

briefdescription.para: Saves the current data as a PNG image file.

parameterdescription.para: The file name for the output PNG image. This should include the file extension (e.g., "output.png").
An integer specifying the colormap to be used for rendering the data. This index refers to a predefined colormap.
A glm::vec4 specifying the bounding box of the data to be included in the image. It is given as {xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax}. The default is {0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f}.
An integer specifying the bit depth of the image. It should be a value defined by OpenCV (e.g., CV_8U for 8-bit unsigned). The default is CV_8U.
A glm::ivec2 specifying the dimensions of the output image. It is given as {width, height}. The default is {512, 512}.

briefdescription.para: Convert path points to a vector of 3D positions.

simplesect.para: Vector of points as (x, y, v).

briefdescription.para: Represents a collection of unordered points in 2D space.

detaileddescription.para: The Cloud class provides functionality to manage and manipulate an unordered set of points in a 2D space. It supports various operations such as adding points, calculating the centroid, merging with other point clouds, and more. This class is useful for applications involving point cloud processing, geometric computations, and spatial analysis.
